title: Piled High in Bento Box Argentinian Lamb Asado
description: A dense, lazy Sunday Argentinian dish featuring lamb, candied peel, and lamination-puff, perfect for a bento box.

ingredients.name: Lamb shoulder
ingredients.notes: Preferably shoulder or leg for better flavor

instructions: Preheat the grill to medium-low heat (around 275°F).
Season the lamb shoulder with salt and black pepper.
Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Sear the lamb shoulder until browned on all sides, about 2-3 minutes per side.
Transfer the lamb to the grill and cook for 2-1/2 to 3 hours, or until tender.
Meanwhile, roll out the lamination puff pastry to a large rectangle.
Spread the candied orange peel over the pastry, leaving a 1-inch border around the edges.
Sprinkle the minced garlic over the orange peel.
Brush the edges of the pastry with water and fold the pastry over the filling, pressing to seal.
Place the pastry on the grill and cook for an additional 10-15 minutes, or until golden brown.
Let the lamb rest for 10 minutes before slicing.
Serve the lamb with the puff pastry and a side of chimichurri sauce.
